summary.intent: Improve the LLM provider/model selection UX by reducing overwhelming model lists, showing most relevant models first, and surfacing probe errors more clearly.
summary.outcome: Implemented collapsible model lists (top 3 visible, expand toggle), recommended model flagging in Rust backend, version-number-based sorting for undated models, inline probe error display with correct error message propagation, and legacy model hiding (>1 year old) in the chat selector.

repo: The project uses three distinct JS files for model card rendering: page-providers.js (settings), onboarding-view.js (onboarding), and providers.js (preferred models modal) — all three need parallel changes for UI consistency

ws-connect.js line 96 calls localizeRpcError() on every WS response before resolving, overwriting server error messages with locale strings based on error.code — this silently discards specific backend error details
ServiceError::Message variants serialize with code UNAVAILABLE, causing all non-Forbidden errors to get the generic 'Service temporarily unavailable' locale string
The project uses mise-managed biome 2.4.6 for CI validation, but npx biome resolves to 0.3.3 — they have different ternary formatting rules, causing CI failures when using npx
models.list() applies legacy filtering and preferred sorting; models.list_all() is used by the settings page and shows everything — the preferred field was missing from list_all responses
The local-validate.sh script must be used for pre-push validation; biome must be run as system biome (2.4.6 via mise), not npx
catalog_to_discovered() helper marks the first N static catalog entries as recommended — provider order in the catalog array determines which models get the recommended badge
Config validation requires updating both schema.rs struct fields AND the validate.rs schema map when adding new config keys

code.finding: localizeRpcError() overwrites frame.error.message with locale string before pending callbacks are called — original server message must be preserved as serverMessage property
All ServiceError::Message variants convert to ErrorShape with code UNAVAILABLE regardless of the actual error content
localizedRpcErrorMessage() ignores error.message when a code-based locale string exists — needed to preserve original message as serverMessage
list_all() was missing the preferred field that list() had — settings page couldn't sort preferred models to top without backend change
parse_model_entry() creates DiscoveredModel from OpenAI API — flagship model IDs (gpt-5.4, o4-mini, o3 without date suffixes) can be pattern-matched here to set recommended:true

workflow: Always run system biome (biome, not npx biome) for formatting — use `which biome` to verify version matches CI (2.4.6)
When adding a field to a Rust struct used in many places, compile early and fix all missing field errors systematically rather than trying to find them all upfront
sed-based bulk edits for adding struct fields to test code are faster than manual edits but can accidentally match non-target structs — verify with a compile check immediately after

friction: The probe error display required fixing three separate code paths (page-providers.js, onboarding-view.js, providers.js) because each had its own model card rendering

The actual error message was being silently replaced by locale strings in ws-connect.js — required tracing through 4 layers (backend log → ServiceError → ErrorShape → localizeRpcError → testModel) to find root cause
biome version mismatch (npx 0.3.3 vs system 2.4.6) caused CI failures on ternary formatting that passed local lint checks

Adding recommended field to ModelInfo required fixing ~20+ struct initializations across providers, tests, swift-bridge, and gateway crates — bulk sed script accidentally modified non-ModelInfo structs (PendingInvoke in server.rs)
open_items: recommended field for GitHub Copilot and OpenAI-compatible providers uses static top-N approach — no intelligence about which models are actually flagships for those providers
The preferred models modal (providers.js) still shows all models without collapse — only onboarding and settings got the top-3 collapse treatment
show_legacy_models config option exists but there's no UI toggle in settings to enable it — users must edit moltis.toml manually
Anthropic models have no createdAt timestamps in the static catalog — version-number sort fallback applies but the catalog order already handles this correctly
popularity-based sorting (pre-selection, before user has usage history) was discussed but only partially implemented via recommended flag — no community/external data source integrated
